description: Probe run to validate run_experiment command acceptance timed out (no benchmark metrics).

asi.hypothesis: run_experiment might accept env-prefixed autoresearch.sh commands
asi.rollback_reason: timeout without metrics; this was a tooling probe, not a real benchmark
asi.next_action_hint: run actual benchmark command and capture METRIC lines

description: Baseline JIT run crashed before METRIC output (BasiliskII aborted in autoresearch.sh with set -e).

asi.hypothesis: Run JIT=true baseline through existing autoresearch.sh with AArch64 experimental configure
asi.rollback_reason: Script exited on emulator abort before emit_metrics; no datapoint captured
asi.next_action_hint: Make autoresearch.sh robust to Basilisk process abort so metrics are emitted even on crash

description: Baseline JIT=true run via autoresearch.sh with AArch64 experimental build mode. Reproduced vm_acquire_reserved assertion (reserved_assert=1).

asi.hypothesis: Current MAP_FIXED low-address path in vm_acquire_mac bypasses reserved pool setup and causes vm_acquire_reserved assert
asi.allocator_observation: Crash occurs in video_open before BOOT_STAGE markers; build succeeds
asi.next_action_hint: Move low-address bias into vm_alloc reservation path and revert vm_acquire_mac to normal vm_acquire flow

description: Move low-address strategy into VM allocator hint path; remove AArch64 MAP_FIXED from vm_acquire_mac; gate JIT block pool off VM_MAP_32BIT on ARM64. JIT run now survives full 20s and reserved assertion is gone.

asi.hypothesis: Initial mmap hint at low base should keep allocator reservation low without violating reserved_buf invariants
asi.result: reserved_assert dropped from 1 to 0; jit_alive_sec improved 0->20
asi.dead_end_avoided: No MAP_FIXED use in vm_acquire_mac, preventing reserved pool bypass
asi.next_action_hint: Validate low-address placement more directly and check deeper boot/JIT progression beyond alive-20s

description: Strengthened low-address allocator: AArch64 first reservation now probes low 4GB with MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE fallback and advances next_address past reserved span. Verified Mac RAM/ROM and JIT cache now map low (mac_ram_low32=1).

asi.hypothesis: Hint-only mmap was insufficient; exact non-replacing low-address probe plus correct next_address advancement should keep all related mappings low
asi.primary_saturation_note: jit_alive_sec already capped at 20s; kept due objective-critical secondary confirmation (mac_ram_low32=1)
asi.evidence: Mac RAM 0x18000000, ROM 0x1c000000, JIT combined region 0x21110000
asi.next_action_hint: Re-initialize primary metric to low-address correctness score to avoid saturation blind spot

description: Simplify low-address allocation: remove MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE probe loop; keep low MAP_BASE hint plus corrected next_address advancement. Behavior unchanged (jit_alive_sec=20, mac_ram_low32=1, no reserved assert).

asi.hypothesis: The key fix may be next_address advancement past reserved span, not MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE probing
asi.result: Equivalent metrics with less allocator complexity
asi.simplification_win: Removed low-4GB exact mapping loop while preserving low mapping in this workload
asi.next_action_hint: Stress-test stability across repeated runs to ensure low mapping remains consistent without MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE

description: Add JIT low-address observability metric (jit_code_low32). Confirmed both RAM and combined popall/cache allocations are below 4GB.

asi.hypothesis: Need explicit visibility that JIT code region stays low, not just RAM
asi.result: jit_code_low32=1 along with mac_ram_low32=1 on stabilized allocator
asi.next_action_hint: Run confirmation repeat(s) to ensure no ASLR-induced regressions across runs

description: Stability confirmation run: metrics unchanged; low-address mapping remains stable.

asi.hypothesis: Low-address behavior should be stable across reruns with ASLR
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement over current best (20s cap) and no new code changes
asi.next_action_hint: Re-initialize primary metric to low-address correctness/completeness score or extend run window for finer signal

description: Tried removing ARM64-specific VM_MAP_32BIT gate for JIT block pool allocator. Regressed to immediate abort (block_pool_fail=1, jit_alive_sec=0).

asi.hypothesis: Low-address allocator fixes might make VM_MAP_32BIT flag unnecessary in block pool path
asi.rollback_reason: Block pool allocation still hard-fails when VM_MAP_32BIT option bit is present on ARM64 despite low mappings
asi.next_action_hint: Keep ARM64 VM_MAP_32BIT gate in block pool allocator; issue is option-bit sanity check, not just actual address placement

description: Removed AArch64 low MAP_BASE hint to test necessity. Process still alive, but RAM/JIT mappings moved back above 4GB (mac_ram_low32=0, jit_code_low32=0), violating low-address objective.

asi.hypothesis: next_address fix alone might keep mappings low even without low MAP_BASE hint
asi.rollback_reason: Low-address requirement regressed; liveness-only metric hid the failure
asi.next_action_hint: Retain AArch64 MAP_BASE low hint and consider reinitializing primary metric to include low-address correctness

description: Reinitialized session with lowaddr_score primary and added lowaddr_score emission to autoresearch.sh. Baseline confirms full low-address correctness under JIT=true.

asi.hypothesis: Need primary metric aligned to low-address objective rather than liveness cap
asi.result: lowaddr_score baseline=100 on current fix set
asi.next_action_hint: Try to improve stage_coverage under same low-address constraints or simplify code without reducing lowaddr_score

description: Tested reverting AArch64 next_address advancement to +size only. Regression: JIT code allocation moved high (jit_code_low32=0), dropping lowaddr_score to 70.

asi.hypothesis: Reserved-pool overlapping hint might be harmless and removable for simpler code
asi.rollback_reason: Without advancing past reserved span, JIT code allocation frequently escapes low 4GB
asi.next_action_hint: Keep next_address = addr + alloc_size in AArch64 branch to preserve low-address code allocations

description: Confirmation rerun: lowaddr_score remains 100 with both low-address metrics stable.

asi.hypothesis: Low-address fix should remain deterministic across reruns
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement beyond current best (already maxed)
asi.next_action_hint: Switch target to boot progression if further optimization is desired; low-address objective appears satisfied
asi.stability_observation: Repeated run kept mac_ram_low32=1 and jit_code_low32=1

name: AArch64 JIT boot progression after low-address fix

description: Baseline for JIT boot progression target: low-address metrics stay good, but stage_coverage_score remains 0 and process exited before 20s in this run.

asi.hypothesis: After low-address fix, next bottleneck is JIT execution correctness rather than mapping
asi.baseline_observation: No boot-stage milestones reached; run ended early despite low mappings
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect log for abort signature (e.g. branch-range or trap) and target next fix there

description: Tried raising AArch64 MAP_BASE hint from 0x10000000 to 0x18000000 to improve JIT stability. No stage_coverage improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Higher low-base hint might reduce early SIGSEGV variance
asi.next_action_hint: Evaluate consistency across reruns before considering MAP_BASE tuning beneficial

description: Repeat of MAP_BASE=0x18000000 experiment showed low-address instability: RAM/JIT mappings moved high (lowaddr_score=40) while stage_coverage remained 0.

asi.hypothesis: MAP_BASE shift might be harmless for low-address goals
asi.rollback_reason: Repeat run disproved stability; low-address correctness regressed
asi.next_action_hint: Keep current MAP_BASE=0x10000000 baseline; avoid this tuning path
asi.variance_note: Same code gave conflicting lowaddr outcomes across runs, indicating high noise/ASLR sensitivity

description: Tried extending run window to 40s via env-parameterized runtime. Stage coverage still 0 and low-address metrics regressed in this run.

asi.hypothesis: Longer runtime might allow boot milestones to appear
asi.rollback_reason: No stage_coverage improvement; also exposed low-address instability under current hint-only allocation
asi.next_action_hint: Revisit allocator robustness (consider MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE probe) before pursuing deeper JIT boot milestones

description: Reinitialized reliability session on lowaddr_score. Baseline at 100 with low RAM/JIT mappings in this run.

asi.hypothesis: Need to harden low-address placement against ASLR variance
asi.baseline_observation: Current hint-only approach can hit 100 but previous runs showed occasional lowaddr regressions
asi.next_action_hint: Reintroduce deterministic low-address probe (MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE) and compare repeat-run stability
